frameworks.name: activity-silent working memory
frameworks.one_line: memories stored in fast synaptic traces without persistent neuronal firing

frameworks.name: persistent activity working memory
frameworks.one_line: active neuronal code maintains unattended memories across the delay

frameworks.name: EEG multivariate decoding
frameworks.one_line: decode memory content from voltage and alpha power around pinging impulses

core_question: Do visual pinging EEG reactivations during memory delays reflect an activity-silent code, or an electrically active one?
key_assumptions: unattended short-term memories can be probed by a visual impulse
decoding presence indexes an active neural code
alpha power and raw voltage tap related but distinct memory signals
manipulations: re-analysis with more statistically powered decoding
decoding from alpha power versus raw voltage
comparison of pre-ping versus post-ping windows for unattended items
key_findings: originally reported absence of pre-ping decoding reflects weak statistical power
memory content is decodable in higher-powered analyses of the same data
alpha-power analyses reveal decoding of unattended items before the ping
interpretations: pinging reactivations occur on top of an active, not silent, memory code
evidence from these EEG datasets does not require activity-silent synaptic storage
limitations_noted: conclusions are constrained to the re-analyzed EEG datasets
